title: EUR/USD Bulls Target 1.1000 as ECB Signals Pause
summary: The euro has surged to multi-month highs against the dollar after ECB officials signalled a prolonged pause in their rate-cut cycle. Technical breakout above 1.0900 opens the path to 1.1000.
content:
The EUR/USD pair extended its rally for a third consecutive session on Friday, climbing to 1.0965 as European Central Bank policymakers reinforced their data-dependent stance and pushed back against aggressive easing expectations.

Key Drivers:

  • ECB Governing Council member Schnabel warned that inflation stickiness in services could delay further cuts.
  • US non-farm payrolls missed estimates at +165k vs +200k expected, weakening dollar demand.
  • Euro-zone PMI data surprised to the upside, with the composite index returning to expansion at 50.4.

Technical Outlook:

Having broken above the 200-day moving average at 1.0855, bulls are now targeting the psychological 1.1000 level. Immediate support sits at 1.0900, while a close below 1.0820 would invalidate the bullish bias. The RSI at 62 leaves room for further upside before overbought territory.

Trading Recommendation: Long EUR/USD on dips towards 1.0900 with a target of 1.1050 and stop loss below 1.0820.
author: Elena Marchetti

title: GBP/USD Retreats as UK Inflation Undershoots Forecasts
summary: Sterling pulled back sharply from 1.2850 after UK CPI printed below consensus, raising expectations of an early Bank of England rate cut. Downside risk towards 1.2600 increases.
content:
The British pound fell over 0.8% against the US dollar on Wednesday after official data showed UK consumer price inflation eased more than anticipated to 2.8% year-on-year in February, down from 3.0% in January and below the 2.9% market consensus.

Key Drivers:

  • UK CPI fell to 2.8% YoY, core CPI dropped to 3.5% from 3.7%.
  • Money markets now price a 70% probability of a May BoE rate cut, up from 45% pre-data.
  • Dollar remained well-bid after Fed Chair Powell reiterated patience on rate cuts.

Technical Outlook:

GBP/USD broke below the short-term uptrend support at 1.2800. Next key support lies at the 50-day moving average near 1.2700. A sustained break below 1.2700 would open the way towards 1.2600. Resistance is now seen at 1.2850.

Trading Recommendation: Short GBP/USD on recoveries towards 1.2800 targeting 1.2650, stop above 1.2870.
author: James Whitmore

title: USD/JPY Consolidates Near 150.00 Ahead of BoJ Decision
summary: The yen is treading water near the critical 150.00 level as traders await the Bank of Japan's policy announcement. A hawkish surprise could trigger sharp yen appreciation.
content:
USD/JPY is consolidating in a narrow 149.50–150.50 range as market participants cautiously position ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting scheduled for next week. The pair has hovered around the psychologically important 150.00 level for the past two weeks.

Key Drivers:

  • BoJ Governor Ueda hinted at further normalisation but stopped short of signalling an imminent hike.
  • Japan's wage growth data for February showed a 3.2% increase, supporting the hawkish narrative.
  • US Treasury yields firmed, providing underlying support for USD/JPY.

Technical Outlook:

The pair is forming a symmetrical triangle pattern. A break above 150.80 could trigger a move to 152.00, while a break below 149.20 would open the path to 148.00. Watch the BoJ press conference closely for directional cues.

Trading Recommendation: Neutral – wait for BoJ resolution before initiating new positions. Favour a hawkish surprise trade: long JPY via short USD/JPY below 149.00 targeting 147.00.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: AUD/USD: China Stimulus Hopes Lift Australian Dollar
summary: The Aussie rallied to 0.6580 as Beijing announced a broad fiscal stimulus package, boosting risk appetite and commodity demand. Further upside eyed toward 0.6650.
content:
AUD/USD advanced 0.6% to 0.6580 on Thursday, its highest level since January, after China's State Council unveiled a CNY 2 trillion fiscal stimulus package targeting infrastructure and domestic consumption. As China is Australia's largest trading partner, the Aussie dollar is highly sensitive to Chinese economic developments.

Key Drivers:

  • China's CNY 2 trillion stimulus package boosted risk sentiment globally.
  • Iron ore prices rose 3.5% to $115/tonne, supporting the Australian dollar.
  • RBA minutes struck a balanced tone, not pre-committing to further cuts.
  • Global equity markets rallied 1.5–2%, boosting high-beta currencies including AUD.

Technical Outlook:

The break above the 0.6550 resistance zone is constructive. The next resistance is at 0.6620 (November 2025 high), followed by 0.6650. Support is at 0.6520. The 14-day RSI has crossed above 55, signalling strengthening momentum.

Trading Recommendation: Long AUD/USD on pullbacks to 0.6540 targeting 0.6650, stop below 0.6490.
author: Sarah Lin

title: Technical Analysis: Dollar Index Approaching Critical Resistance
summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) is testing major resistance at 105.00. A decisive rejection could herald a broader dollar downtrend, reshaping FX landscape heading into Q2 2026.
content:
The US Dollar Index (DXY) is at a crossroads. After a 3% rally from early-February lows, the index is testing the 105.00 level, a zone that served as both support and resistance multiple times over the past 18 months.

Technical Picture:

  • Resistance: 105.00 (key level), 105.60 (200-week MA)
  • Support: 103.80 (50-day MA), 103.00 (trend support)
  • RSI (14): 58 – approaching but not yet overbought
  • MACD: Positive crossover in place but momentum slowing

Scenario Analysis:

Bullish Dollar Scenario (probability 35%): A clean break above 105.00 confirmed by a daily close opens the path to 106.50. This scenario would pressure EUR/USD below 1.0800 and lift USD/JPY above 152.00.

Bearish Dollar Scenario (probability 65%): Rejection at 105.00 and reversal below 104.50 would signal the end of the corrective rally. Target 102.50 over 4–6 weeks. EUR/USD could regain 1.1100.

Key Events to Watch: US CPI (Tuesday), FOMC minutes (Wednesday), Fed Chair Powell speech (Friday).
author: Marcus Reeves

title: Market Outlook: Fed Pivot Timeline Shifts – What It Means for FX
summary: Stronger US data has pushed the first Fed rate cut expectation back to September 2026. We examine how this shift reshapes the FX landscape across major pairs and EM currencies.
content:
The repricing of Fed expectations following a string of upside US economic surprises – from robust non-farm payrolls to sticky CPI – has materially shifted the first cut expectation from June to September 2026. This has significant implications for global FX markets.

Impact by Currency Pair:

  • EUR/USD: The rate differential compression trade is delayed. EUR/USD upside capped near 1.1000 near-term. Look for range-trading between 1.0800–1.1000 for Q2.
  • GBP/USD: BoE likely to cut before Fed now. Sterling faces additional headwinds; 1.2500 downside risk in H1 2026.
  • USD/JPY: Elevated US rates keep upside pressure on pair, but BoJ normalisation should cap gains at 153.00. Range: 148.00–153.00.
  • AUD/USD: Chinese stimulus provides an offsetting positive. AUD resilient vs USD despite dollar strength. Range: 0.6400–0.6700.
  • EM Currencies: Delayed Fed cuts are negative for EM; expect continued pressure on BRL, TRY, and ZAR.

Macro Calendar Highlights: US CPI (March 18), ECB meeting (March 20), Fed meeting (March 22), BoJ decision (March 19). These four events will be critical in resetting expectations.
author: Dr. Alexandra Ross

title: USD/CHF: Safe-Haven Demand Supports Franc Amid Geopolitical Tensions
summary: Renewed geopolitical uncertainty in Eastern Europe has driven safe-haven flows into the Swiss franc. USD/CHF dipped below parity – is it heading toward 0.9800?
content:
The Swiss franc strengthened across the board on Monday as geopolitical tensions flared in Eastern Europe, driving traditional safe-haven demand into CHF and JPY. USD/CHF fell below the psychologically important parity level (1.0000) to trade at 0.9930.

Key Drivers:

  • Geopolitical risk premium returned after ceasefire negotiations stalled.
  • SNB's December statement reiterated willingness to intervene in FX market if needed – but current levels may not yet trigger action.
  • EUR/CHF fell to 1.0750, approaching SNB's estimated comfort floor near 1.0650.

Technical Outlook:

USD/CHF has broken below the ascending trend channel originating from October lows. The next support is at 0.9880 (2025 low), followed by 0.9800. A recovery above 1.0000 is needed to neutralise the bearish signal. SNB intervention risk increases below 0.9850.

Trading Recommendation: Cautious short USD/CHF below 0.9980 targeting 0.9880. Reduce exposure ahead of key geopolitical updates. Stop loss at 1.0050.
author: Hans Weber

title: Fundamental Analysis: Why EUR/USD Could Break Above 1.1200 in Q2 2026
summary: A confluence of fundamental tailwinds – ECB pause, US fiscal concerns, and euro-zone recovery – presents a structural case for EUR/USD to reclaim levels not seen since early 2024.
content:
Looking beyond near-term noise, a structural case is building for a sustained EUR/USD rally toward 1.1200 by the end of Q2 2026. This note outlines the three fundamental pillars supporting this view.

1. ECB Rate Pause Creates Relative Yield Support

With the ECB signalling a pause after four 25bp cuts, the EUR-USD 2-year swap spread has narrowed significantly, from -150bp in January to -95bp today. Should US data soften and the Fed begin cutting in September, this spread could compress to -50bp, a level historically consistent with EUR/USD near 1.1000–1.1200.

2. US Fiscal Concerns Weigh on Long-Term Dollar Outlook

The US Congressional Budget Office projects the federal deficit to remain above 5% of GDP through 2028. With the debt ceiling debate intensifying and Moody's recent outlook change to negative, the structural case for USD weakness is building. Historical precedent (2003–2004, 2017–2018) shows fiscal concerns eventually weigh on reserve currency demand.

3. Euro-zone Cyclical Recovery Underway

After three quarters of stagnation, euro-zone GDP grew 0.4% in Q4 2025. Leading indicators suggest this recovery is broadening: German manufacturing orders rose 2.1% MoM in January, French consumer confidence hit a 2-year high, and Spanish unemployment fell to 10.8%, its lowest since 2008.

Risks to the View: A resurgence of euro-zone energy price inflation, political instability in France or Italy, or a US growth acceleration surprising to the upside.

12-Month Target: EUR/USD 1.1200. Build long positions on dips below 1.0900.
author: Elena Marchetti
